functional_purpose: Processes PAS-origin HL7v2 patient, merge, referral, appointment, and did-not-attend events and submits mapped XML to OpenEyes PASAPI. It also decodes ReportFile OBX content into a Mirth attachment.
business_process: Patient demographic synchronisation, patient merge/referral handling, clinic appointment status maintenance, and did-not-attend recording for the Newmedica practices.
source_system: Upstream Mirth channel carrying PAS-origin HL7v2
ack_behaviour: No network ACK is produced by this Channel Reader source.
main_filters: Secondary patient and patient merge destinations accept A40 only.
Appointment PUT accepts A01, A02, A03, A04, A05, A11, A12, A13, S12, S13, S14, or S26 and rejects ReportFile OBX.
Appointment DELETE accepts A38 or S15.
Did-not-attend accepts S26 and rejects ReportFile OBX.
OBX attachment destination accepts ReportFile; its Cataract filter is disabled.
main_transformer_stages: Map practice identifier from PV1 location, transform HL7 demographics to PASAPI Patient XML, and derive hospital identifiers.
Parse PASAPI patient response, mark actionable warnings as errors, and store PatientId in message-scoped channelMap.
Map referral, appointment, appointment status, merge, and did-not-attend payload values from HL7 segments.
Decode ReportFile OBX payloads into Mirth attachments.
error_handling: Transformer scripts catch mapping exceptions and log errors. PASAPI patient response warnings other than missing GP or Practice set the response status to error.
retry_behaviour: Destination retryCount is 0 with a 10000 ms configured retry interval.
queue_behaviour: Source buffer size is 1000; all destination queues are disabled.
logging: Logs transformer mapping exceptions and a debug message for OBX document handling. The channel stores production message content, so operational logs/message storage can contain PII.

evidence.claim: Channel Reader accepts HL7v2 from an upstream Mirth channel; enabled HTTP destinations send patient, referral, appointment, merge, and did-not-attend data to OpenEyes PASAPI V1 and V3.
evidence.location: Newmedica, PAS In LOCAL-209-0, 60ae6aac-a8d0-46fc-a873-ee4c65a3952e, Newmedica/PAS.xml, source Connector lines 7-116; HTTP destination connectors lines 119-640, 645-1935, 1936-2661, and 2924-3212

evidence.claim: The PASAPI transformers map HL7 patient and visit content to XML, derive hospital and secondary hospital numbers, patient identifier, appointment values, and parse PASAPI responses for warnings and PatientId.
evidence.location: Newmedica, PAS In LOCAL-209-0, 60ae6aac-a8d0-46fc-a873-ee4c65a3952e, Newmedica/PAS.xml, destination transformer/response transformer PASAPI - Secondary Patient lines 180-625; PASAPI - Patient lines 706-1158; PASAPI - Patient Referral and Clinic List - PUT lines 1160-2163

evidence.claim: Destination filters route A40 to secondary patient and patient merge, A38/S15 to appointment deletion, appointment events A01/A02/A03/A04/A05/A11/A12/A13/S12/S13/S14/S26 to appointment PUT, S26 to did-not-attend, and suppress ReportFile on HTTP appointment routes.
evidence.location: Newmedica, PAS In LOCAL-209-0, 60ae6aac-a8d0-46fc-a873-ee4c65a3952e, Newmedica/PAS.xml, filters PASAPI - Secondary Patient lines 626-639, Clinic List - PUT lines 2102-2163, Clinic List - DELETE lines 2389-2405, PASAPI - Patient Merge lines 2644-2657, Clinic List - DNA lines 3163-3209

evidence.claim: All HTTP destinations have destination queue disabled and retryCount 0, use preemptive HTTP Basic authentication with a redacted password, and response warning handling marks non-GP/non-Practice warnings as errors. The OBX JavaScript destination decodes ReportFile content to a Mirth attachment.
evidence.location: Newmedica, PAS In LOCAL-209-0, 60ae6aac-a8d0-46fc-a873-ee4c65a3952e, Newmedica/PAS.xml, HTTP destination connector properties including PASAPI - Secondary Patient lines 124-172 and Clinic List - DNA lines 2929-2985; PASAPI response transformer lines 559-594; OBX document transformer lines 2665-2922

functional_purpose: Processes PAS-origin HL7v2 patient, merge, referral, appointment, and did-not-attend events and submits mapped XML to OpenEyes PASAPI. It also decodes ReportFile OBX content into a Mirth attachment.
business_process: Patient demographic synchronisation, patient merge/referral handling, clinic appointment status maintenance, and did-not-attend recording for the Newmedica practices.
source_system: Upstream Mirth channel carrying PAS-origin HL7v2
ack_behaviour: No network ACK is produced by this Channel Reader source.
main_filters: Secondary patient and patient merge destinations accept A40 only.
Appointment PUT accepts A01, A02, A03, A04, A05, A11, A12, A13, S12, S13, S14, or S26 and rejects ReportFile OBX.
Appointment DELETE accepts A38 or S15.
Did-not-attend accepts S26 and rejects ReportFile OBX.
OBX attachment destination accepts ReportFile; its Cataract filter is disabled.
main_transformer_stages: Map practice identifier from PV1 location, transform HL7 demographics to PASAPI Patient XML, and derive hospital identifiers.
Parse PASAPI patient response, mark actionable warnings as errors, and store PatientId in message-scoped channelMap.
Map referral, appointment, appointment status, merge, and did-not-attend payload values from HL7 segments.
Decode ReportFile OBX payloads into Mirth attachments.
error_handling: Transformer scripts catch mapping exceptions and log errors. PASAPI patient response warnings other than missing GP or Practice set the response status to error.
retry_behaviour: Destination retryCount is 0 with a 10000 ms configured retry interval.
queue_behaviour: Source buffer size is 1000; all destination queues are disabled.
logging: Logs transformer mapping exceptions and a debug message for OBX document handling. The channel stores production message content, so operational logs/message storage can contain PII.
